questTitle: P4 Math Revision: Units & Equal Concept (Part 1)
subject: Mathematics
description: Master word problems using units and equal concepts! This worksheet covers comparison problems and equal sharing problems. Draw models to help you solve! Read each question carefully.

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 1: Understanding Units (Times As Many)
questions.question: Lisa has 5 times as many sweets as Amy. If we let Amy's sweets be represented as 1 unit, how many units would represent Lisa's sweets?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 1: Understanding Units (Times As Many)
questions.question: Amy has 1 unit of sweets and Lisa has 5 units of sweets. How many units do they have altogether?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 1: Understanding Units (Times As Many)
questions.question: Amy and Lisa have 6 units of sweets altogether, which equals 48 sweets in total. How many sweets does 1 unit represent?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 1: Understanding Units (Times As Many)
questions.question: If 1 unit equals 8 sweets, how many sweets does Lisa have if she has 5 units?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 1: Understanding Units (Times As Many) - Test Question
questions.question: Marcus has 4 times as many stickers as Nadia. They have 60 stickers altogether. How many stickers does Marcus have?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 2: Comparison - One Amount Unchanged
questions.question: Lisa has 4 times as many sweets as Amy. Amy's mother then gives Amy some more sweets, but Lisa's amount stays the same. What happens to the total number of sweets they have?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 2: Comparison - One Amount Unchanged
questions.question: At first, Lisa has 4 units and Amy has 1 unit. After Amy receives 18 more sweets, Lisa now has 2 times as many sweets as Amy. If Lisa still has 4 units, how many units does Amy have now?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 2: Comparison - One Amount Unchanged
questions.question: Amy started with 1 unit and now has 2 units. The increase of 1 unit came from the 18 sweets she received. How many sweets does 1 unit represent?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 2: Comparison - One Amount Unchanged
questions.question: If 1 unit equals 18 sweets, and Lisa has 4 units, how many sweets did Lisa have at first?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 2: Comparison - One Amount Unchanged - Test Question
questions.question: Ryan has 6 times as many cards as Siti. Siti's father gives her 24 more cards. Now Ryan has 3 times as many cards as Siti. How many cards did Ryan have at first?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 3: Equal at First - Transfer
questions.question: Ken and Danny have an equal number of cards at first. If Ken gives Danny 54 cards, what happens to Ken's amount compared to before?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 3: Equal at First - Transfer
questions.question: Ken and Danny started with equal amounts. Ken gives Danny 54 cards. By how much does the difference between Danny's and Ken's cards increase?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 3: Equal at First - Transfer
questions.question: After Ken gives Danny 54 cards, Danny has 4 times as many cards as Ken. If Ken now has 1 part, how many parts does Danny have?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 3: Equal at First - Transfer
questions.question: Danny has 4 parts and Ken has 1 part. The difference between them is 3 parts, which equals 108 cards. How many cards does 1 part represent?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 3: Equal at First - Transfer - Test Question
questions.question: Priya and Qian have an equal number of beads at first. After Priya gave Qian 42 beads, Qian has 5 times as many beads as Priya. How many beads did they have altogether at first?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 4: More Than - Before and After
questions.question: Sarah has 24 more stickers than Mei. If Mei has 1 unit of stickers, how would you represent Sarah's stickers in terms of units?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 4: More Than - Before and After
questions.question: Sarah gives some stickers to Mei. After that, Mei has 3 times as many stickers as Sarah. If Sarah now has 1 part, how many parts does Mei have?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 4: More Than - Before and After
questions.question: Together, Sarah and Mei now have 4 parts of stickers. If Mei had 42 stickers at first and Sarah had 24 more than Mei, what was the total number of stickers at first?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 4: More Than - Before and After
questions.question: Sarah and Mei have 108 stickers in total, which equals 4 parts. How many stickers does 1 part represent?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 4: More Than - Before and After - Test Question
questions.question: Ahmad has 18 more erasers than Bella. After Ahmad gives some erasers to Bella, Bella has 2 times as many erasers as Ahmad. If Bella had 36 erasers at first, how many erasers did Ahmad give to Bella?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 5: Times As Many - Transfer to Equal
questions.question: Ali has 3 times as many marbles as Ben. If Ben has 1 unit of marbles, how many units of marbles does Ali have?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 5: Times As Many - Transfer to Equal
questions.question: Ali has 3 units and Ben has 1 unit. How many more units does Ali have than Ben?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 5: Times As Many - Transfer to Equal
questions.question: Ali gives Ben 14 marbles and they now have the same number of marbles. When Ali gives Ben 14 marbles, by how much does the difference between them decrease?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 5: Times As Many - Transfer to Equal
questions.question: Ali had 2 units more than Ben at first. After Ali gives Ben 14 marbles, they have the same number. The difference of 2 units equals 28 marbles. How many marbles does 1 unit represent?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 5: Times As Many - Transfer to Equal - Test Question
questions.question: Cheng has 5 times as many pencils as David. After Cheng gives David 24 pencils, they have the same number of pencils. How many pencils did Cheng have at first?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 6: Equal - Both Give Away
questions.question: Lily and Rose have an equal number of beads at first. Lily gives away 15 beads and Rose gives away 36 beads. Who has more beads remaining?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 6: Equal - Both Give Away
questions.question: Lily and Rose started with equal amounts. Lily gives away 15 beads and Rose gives away 36 beads. What is the difference between Lily's and Rose's remaining beads?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 6: Equal - Both Give Away
questions.question: After giving away beads, Lily has 4 times as many beads as Rose. If Rose now has 1 part, how many parts does Lily have?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 6: Equal - Both Give Away
questions.question: Lily has 4 parts and Rose has 1 part. The difference of 3 parts equals 21 beads. How many beads does 1 part represent?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 6: Equal - Both Give Away - Test Question
questions.question: Grace and Hannah have an equal number of coins at first. Grace gives away 10 coins and Hannah gives away 50 coins. After that, Grace has 5 times as many coins as Hannah. How many coins did they have altogether at first?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 7: Combined Units - Changes After
questions.question: Cindy has twice as many stickers as Diana. If Diana has 1 unit, how many units does Cindy have?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 7: Combined Units - Changes After
questions.question: Cindy has 2 units and Diana has 1 unit. Together they have 45 stickers. How many stickers does 1 unit represent?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 7: Combined Units - Changes After
questions.question: Cindy has 30 stickers and Diana has 15 stickers. Cindy buys 6 more stickers and Diana gives away 3 stickers. How many stickers does Cindy have now?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 7: Combined Units - Changes After
questions.question: Cindy now has 36 stickers and Diana now has 12 stickers. How many times as many stickers does Cindy have compared to Diana?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 7: Combined Units - Changes After - Test Question
questions.question: Ethan has 3 times as many cards as Farah. Ethan then buys 12 more cards while Farah gives away 4 cards. Now Ethan has 5 times as many cards as Farah. How many cards did Ethan have at first?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 8: Mixed Review - Units Concept
questions.question: Peter has 4 times as many coins as Queenie. If Queenie has 1 unit, how many units do they have altogether?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 8: Mixed Review - Units Concept
questions.question: Peter and Queenie have 5 units altogether, which equals 60 coins. How many coins does 1 unit represent?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 8: Mixed Review - Units Concept
questions.question: If 1 unit equals 12 coins and Peter has 4 units, how many coins does Peter have?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 8: Mixed Review - Units Concept
questions.question: Queenie has 12 coins (1 unit). Peter has 48 coins (4 units). If Peter gives Queenie 18 coins, how many coins does each person have now?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 8: Mixed Review - Units Concept - Test Question
questions.question: Rachel has 5 times as many stamps as Samuel. They have 72 stamps altogether. Rachel then gives Samuel some stamps and they now have equal amounts. How many stamps did Rachel give to Samuel?
